an7.68:0.3: One With a Sense of Dhamma
an7.68:1.1: “A monk endowed with these seven qualities is deserving of gifts, deserving of hospitality, deserving of offerings, deserving of respect, an unexcelled field of merit for the world.
an7.68:1.3: There is the case where a monk is one with a sense of Dhamma, a sense of meaning, a sense of himself, a sense of moderation, a sense of time, a sense of social gatherings, & a sense of distinctions among individuals.
an7.68:2.1: “And how is a monk one with a sense of Dhamma?
an7.68:2.2: There is the case where a monk knows the Dhamma:
an7.68:2.3: dialogues, narratives of mixed prose and verse, explanations, verses, spontaneous exclamations, quotations, birth stories, amazing events, question & answer sessions [the earliest classifications of the Buddha’s teachings].
an7.68:2.4: If he didn’t know the Dhamma—
an7.68:2.5: dialogues, narratives of mixed prose and verse, explanations, verses, spontaneous exclamations, quotations, birth stories, amazing events, question & answer sessions—he wouldn’t be said to be one with a sense of Dhamma.
an7.68:2.6: So it’s because he does know the Dhamma—
an7.68:2.7: dialogues… question & answer sessions—that he is said to be one with a sense of Dhamma.
an7.68:2.8: This is one with a sense of Dhamma.
an7.68:3.1: “And how is a monk one with a sense of meaning?
an7.68:3.2: There is the case where a monk knows the meaning of this & that statement—
an7.68:3.3: ‘This is the meaning of that statement; that is the meaning of this.’
an7.68:3.4: If he didn’t know the meaning of this & that statement—
an7.68:3.5: ‘This is the meaning of that statement; that is the meaning of this’—he wouldn’t be said to be one with a sense of meaning.
an7.68:3.6: So it’s because he does know the meaning of this & that statement—
an7.68:3.7: ‘This is the meaning of that statement; that is the meaning of this’—that he is said to be one with a sense of meaning.
an7.68:3.8: This is one with a sense of Dhamma & a sense of meaning.
an7.68:4.1: “And how is a monk one with a sense of himself?
an7.68:4.2: There is the case where a monk knows himself:
an7.68:4.3: ‘This is how far I have come in conviction, virtue, learning, generosity, discernment, quick-wittedness.’
an7.68:4.5: ‘This is how far I have come in conviction, virtue, learning, generosity, discernment, quick-wittedness’—he wouldn’t be said to be one with a sense of himself.
an7.68:4.6: So it’s because he does know himself—
an7.68:4.7: ‘This is how far I have come in conviction, virtue, learning, generosity, discernment, quick-wittedness’—that he is said to be one with a sense of himself.
an7.68:4.8: This is one with a sense of Dhamma, a sense of meaning, & a sense of himself.
an7.68:5.1: “And how is a monk one with a sense of moderation?
an7.68:5.2: There is the case where a monk knows moderation in accepting robes, almsfood, lodgings, & medicinal requisites for curing the sick.
an7.68:5.3: If he didn’t know moderation in accepting robes, almsfood, lodgings, & medicinal requisites for curing the sick, he wouldn’t be said to be one with a sense of moderation.
an7.68:5.4: So it’s because he does know moderation in accepting robes, almsfood, lodgings, & medicinal requisites for curing the sick, that he is said to be one with a sense of moderation.
an7.68:5.5: This is one with a sense of Dhamma, a sense of meaning, a sense of himself, & a sense of moderation.
an7.68:6.1: “And how is a monk one with a sense of time?
an7.68:6.2: There is the case where a monk knows the time:
an7.68:6.3: ‘This is the time for recitation; this, the time for questioning; this, the time for making an effort (in meditation); this, the time for seclusion.’
an7.68:6.4: If he didn’t know the time—
an7.68:6.5: ‘This is the time for recitation; this, the time for questioning; this, the time for making an effort; this, the time for seclusion’—he wouldn’t be said to be one with a sense of time.
an7.68:6.6: So it’s because he does know the time—
an7.68:6.7: ‘This is the time for recitation; this, the time for questioning; this, the time for making an effort; this, the time for seclusion’—that he is said to be one with a sense of time.
an7.68:6.8: This is one with a sense of Dhamma, a sense of meaning, a sense of himself, a sense of moderation, & a sense of time.
an7.68:7.1: “And how is a monk one with a sense of social gatherings?
an7.68:7.2: There is the case where a monk knows his social gathering:
an7.68:7.3: ‘This is a social gathering of noble warriors; this, a social gathering of brahmans; this, a social gathering of householders; this, a social gathering of contemplatives;
an7.68:7.4: here one should approach them in this way, stand in this way, act in this way, sit in this way, speak in this way, stay silent in this way.’
an7.68:7.5: If he didn’t know his social gathering—
an7.68:7.6: ‘This is a social gathering of noble warriors; this, a social gathering of brahmans; this, a social gathering of householders; this, a social gathering of contemplatives; here one should approach them in this way, stand in this way, act in this way, sit in this way, speak in this way, stay silent in this way’—
an7.68:7.7: he wouldn’t be said to be one with a sense of social gatherings.
an7.68:7.8: So it’s because he does know his social gathering—
an7.68:7.9: ‘This is a social gathering of noble warriors; this, a social gathering of brahmans; this, a social gathering of householders; this, a social gathering of contemplatives; here one should approach them in this way, stand in this way, act in this way, sit in this way, speak in this way, stay silent in this way’—
an7.68:7.10: that he is said to be one with a sense of social gatherings.
an7.68:7.11: This is one with a sense of Dhamma, a sense of meaning, a sense of himself, a sense of moderation, a sense of time, & a sense of social gatherings.
an7.68:8.1: “And how is a monk one with a sense of distinctions among individuals?
an7.68:8.2: There is the case where people are known to a monk in terms of two categories.
an7.68:8.4: one who wants to see noble ones and one who doesn’t—
an7.68:8.5: the one who doesn’t want to see noble ones
an7.68:8.6: is to be criticized for that reason,
an7.68:8.7: the one who does want to see noble ones
an7.68:8.8: is, for that reason, to be praised.
an7.68:9.1: “Of two people who want to see noble ones—
an7.68:9.2: one who wants to hear the true Dhamma and one who doesn’t—
an7.68:9.3: the one who doesn’t want to hear the true Dhamma
an7.68:9.4: is to be criticized for that reason,
an7.68:9.5: the one who does want to hear the true Dhamma
an7.68:9.6: is, for that reason, to be praised.
an7.68:10.1: “Of two people who want to hear the true Dhamma—
an7.68:10.2: one who listens with an attentive ear and one who listens without an attentive ear—
an7.68:10.3: the one who listens without an attentive ear
an7.68:10.4: is to be criticized for that reason,
an7.68:10.5: the one who listens with an attentive ear
an7.68:10.6: is, for that reason, to be praised.
an7.68:11.1: “Of two people who listen with an attentive ear—
an7.68:11.2: one who, having listened to the Dhamma, remembers it, and one who doesn’t—
an7.68:11.3: the one who, having listened to the Dhamma, doesn’t remember it
an7.68:11.4: is to be criticized for that reason,
an7.68:11.5: the one who, having listened to the Dhamma, does remember the Dhamma
an7.68:11.6: is, for that reason, to be praised.
an7.68:12.1: “Of two people who, having listened to the Dhamma, remember it—
an7.68:12.2: one who explores the meaning of the Dhamma he has remembered and one who doesn’t—
an7.68:12.3: the one who doesn’t explore the meaning of the Dhamma he has remembered
an7.68:12.4: is to be criticized for that reason,
an7.68:12.5: the one who does explore the meaning of the Dhamma he has remembered
an7.68:12.6: is, for that reason, to be praised.
an7.68:13.1: “Of two people who explore the meaning of the Dhamma they have remembered—
an7.68:13.2: one who practices the Dhamma in line with the Dhamma, having a sense of Dhamma, having a sense of meaning, and one who doesn’t—
an7.68:13.3: the one who doesn’t practice the Dhamma in line with the Dhamma, having a sense of Dhamma, having a sense of meaning,
an7.68:13.4: is to be criticized for that reason,
an7.68:13.5: the one who does practice the Dhamma in line with the Dhamma, having a sense of Dhamma, having a sense of meaning
an7.68:13.6: is, for that reason, to be praised.
an7.68:14.1: “Of two people who practice the Dhamma in line with the Dhamma, having a sense of Dhamma, having a sense of meaning—
an7.68:14.2: one who practices for both his own benefit and that of others, and one who practices for his own benefit but not that of others—
an7.68:14.3: the one who practices for his own benefit but not that of others
an7.68:14.4: is to be criticized for that reason,
an7.68:14.5: the one who practices for both his own benefit and that of others
an7.68:14.6: is, for that reason, to be praised.
an7.68:15.1: “This is how people are known to a monk in terms of two categories.
an7.68:16.1: And this is how a monk is one with a sense of distinctions among individuals.
an7.68:16.2: “A monk endowed with these seven qualities is deserving of gifts, deserving of hospitality, deserving of offerings, deserving of respect, an unexcelled field of merit for the world.”
